prompt: Predict the value. Fresh numbers every time — go for speed and automaticity.
Predict the remainder. Fresh numbers every time — go for speed and automaticity.

concept: positive and negative indexing with at()
prompt: Predict the element. Watch for negative indices (arr.at). Fresh data every time.
Predict the slice (in quotes). Fresh bounds every time — build slicing into muscle memory.
Predict the array: doubles of the even values. Fresh data every time.

concept: max of an array by hand
prompt: Implement `biggest(a)` returning the largest element — by hand, no `Math.max`. Fresh data every time.
Predict the value. Map.get returns undefined for a key that is not present. Fresh queries every time.
Predict the sorted array. The (a, b) => a - b comparator sorts numerically (default sort is lexicographic). Fresh data every time.
Predict the value. || falls back on any falsy left side; ?? falls back only on null or undefined. Watch what happens when the left is 0. Fresh each time.
Predict the array. Destructuring swaps both bindings in one step, with no temp variable. Fresh numbers every time.
Predict the object. Spreading first, then a later key, lets the later key win. Fresh values every time.

concept: Object.keys returns keys in insertion order
prompt: Predict the array of keys (in quotes). Object.keys lists string keys in insertion order. Fresh objects every time.
Predict the value. Optional chaining stops and yields undefined the moment a link is missing. Fresh path every time.

concept: flatMap maps then flattens one level
prompt: Predict the array. flatMap maps each item to an array, then flattens one level. Fresh data every time.

concept: count frequencies into a Map by hand
prompt: Implement `tally(items)` returning a Map from each item to how many times it appears. Fresh data every time.
Implement `reverseStr(s)` returning `s` with its characters in reverse order, without using `.reverse`. Fresh data every time.
Implement `countVowels(s)` returning how many characters of `s` are one of a, e, i, o, u. Fresh data every time.
Implement `isPalindrome(s)` returning `true` when `s` reads the same forward and backward, else `false`. Fresh data every time.
Implement `flattenOne(a)` where `a` is an array of arrays, returning one array with the inner elements in order, without using `.flat`. Fresh data every time.
Implement `dedupe(a)` returning a new array with duplicate values removed, keeping the first occurrence of each in order. Fresh data every time.

Implement `fizzbuzz(n)` returning an array of length `n`. For each i from 1 to n use the string "Fizz" if i is a multiple of 3, "Buzz" if a multiple of 5, "FizzBuzz" if both, otherwise the number i as a string. Fresh data every time.
Implement `sumOfDigits(n)` returning the sum of the decimal digits of the non-negative integer `n`. Fresh data every time.
Implement `runningTotal(a)` returning an array of the same length where each element is the sum of `a` up to and including that index. Fresh data every time.
Implement `twoSum(a, target)` returning a pair of distinct indices `[i, j]` such that `a[i] + a[j] === target`. A valid pair is guaranteed to exist. Fresh data every time.

Implement `binarySearch(a, target)` where `a` is sorted ascending with distinct values, returning the index of `target` or -1 if absent, without using `.indexOf` or `.includes`. Fresh data every time.
Implement `maxSubArray(a)` returning the largest sum of any non-empty contiguous run of `a`. Fresh data every time.
Implement `moveZeroes(a)` returning an array with all zeros moved to the end, keeping the order of the non-zero values. Fresh data every time.
Implement `validParens(s)` returning `true` when every bracket in `s` (from ()[]{}) is closed by the correct type in the correct order, else `false`. Fresh data every time.
Implement `maxWindowSum(a, k)` returning the largest sum of any `k` consecutive elements of `a`. Fresh data every time.
